patterns.name: Identical UpdateAkSk client wrapper duplicated across kv/object/stream C-API wrappers
patterns.rationale: SCUpdateAkSk, OCUpdateAkSk and StreamUpdateAkSk have byte-identical bodies: same reinterpret_cast of the opaque client pointer, the same (buggy) redundant `accessKey.assign(...)` right after constructing `accessKey`, the same SensitiveValue construction, the same UpdateAkSk call and the same error-to-StatusC handling. Only the function name and the templated client type differ. This is copy-paste that should be a single templated/shared helper; the redundant assign is duplicated three times, so a fix must be applied in three places.

patterns.name: Identical CreateClient trace-and-delegate wrapper (kv and object C-API)
patterns.rationale: KVCreateClient and OCCreateClient are byte-for-byte identical: same 17-parameter signature, same TraceGuard, and the same forwarding call to CreateObjectClient with the identical argument list. The two functions differ only in name and return type alias; the entire body is duplicated and should be a single shared helper.

patterns.name: Repeated public-op wrapper boilerplate: TraceGuard + AccessRecorder + Execute + Record
patterns.rationale: Every public C-API op is a thin wrapper that repeats the identical scaffold: construct a TraceGuard, construct an AccessRecorder for the op's key, declare a RequestParam, call the corresponding *Execute* worker, then accessPoint.Record(rc.code, "0", reqParam, rc.errMsg) and return rc. OCGIncreaseRef, OCDeccreaseRef and OCReleaseGRefs (and many peers across kv/object/stream) are structurally identical up to the AccessRecorderKey and the delegated Execute call, making this a strong candidate for a macro or template wrapper.
